title: Cytisus jacquinianus
intro: Cytisus jacquinianus, also known as the Broom or Scotch Broom, is a deciduous shrub belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in the treatment of respiratory issues and skin conditions. Cytisus jacquinianus contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cytisus jacquinianus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and bronchitis.
taxonomy: Cytisus jacquinianus belongs to the family Fabaceae. This plant is a species of broom in the genus Cytisus. Cytisus jacquinianus is native to the Mediterranean region. It is a flowering plant, known for its yellow flowers. The species is often classified within the subgenus Cytisus.

side_effects: Cytisus jacquinianus, also known as the Albanian laburnum, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine. However, its use may cause side effects due to the presence of toxic compounds. Consuming the plant's flowers, leaves, or seeds can cause symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea.
